sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.

You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 55, level 2.
Valid actions right now: MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: - Magenta square at (31-32, 28-29) is touching the blue circle at its bottom-left corner - Blue circle spans rows 23-31, cols 29-37 - Need to test if overlapping with blue circle triggers something - Used 24 actions, target is 42
- Plan: Move the magenta square one more time to overlap with the blue circle and see if something happens.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 25 of about 42 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The magenta square moved from (31-32, 28-29) to (30-31, 27-28). It's now at rows 30-31, cols 27-28. The blue circle is at rows 23-31, cols 29-37. So the square is now overlapping with the blue circle's bottom row (31) but its columns (27-28) are still to the left of the blue circle's left column (29).
Looking at the image, the magenta square is now partially overlapping with the blue circle. Let me move it one more time to see if it fully enters the blue circle.
Actually, I'm starting to wonder if the goal is to move all magenta squares into the blue circle. But that would take many actions, and I'm already at 25 out of 42.
Let me think about this differently. The blue circle is at rows 23-31, cols 29-37. The magenta square is at rows 30-31, cols 27-28. To fully enter the blue circle, the square needs to be at rows 23-31 and cols 29-37. But the square is 2x2, so it needs to be at rows 30-31 and cols 29-30 to be fully inside the blue circle.
Wait, but the square moves up-left, so it would move to (29-30, 26-27), which is further left and up. That would move it away from the blue circle's right side.
Hmm, maybe the goal is not to move the magenta squares into the blue circle, but to position them around it in a specific pattern.
Actually, let me reconsider the whole approach. I've been moving one magenta square towards the blue circle, but maybe the goal is different.
Let me look at the current state:
- Magenta squares at: (30-31, 27-28), (35-36, 16-17), (54-55, 45-46), (56-57, 15-16)
- Blue circle at (23-31, 29-37)
- Yellow 4x4 square at (3-6, 30-33)
- Colored squares in top bar: S (1,1), M (1,5), p (1,9), Y (1,13)
Maybe the goal is to move the magenta squares to specific positions that match the colored squares in the top bar. But I'm not sure how the colors relate to positions.
Actually, let me try a different approach. Let me move the magenta square one more time to see if it fully enters the blue circle, and if that triggers something.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model:
- Magenta square at (30-31, 27-28) is partially overlapping with blue circle
- Blue circle at rows 23-31, cols 29-37
- Square needs to move right to fully enter blue circle, but it moves up-left
- Used 25 actions, target is 42
Plan: Move the magenta square one more time and see if overlapping triggers something. If not, reconsider the goal.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move the magenta square at (30-31, 27-28) one more time
# Top-left is (30, 27)
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 30, 'col': 27}])

after = current_frame.segmentation
m_positions = []
for n in after['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 4:
        b = n['boundary']
        r = (min([p[0] for p in b]), max([p[0] for p in b]), min([p[1] for p in b]), max([p[1] for p in b]))
        m_positions.append(r)

print(f"Magenta positions: {sorted(m_positions)}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

# Check if magenta square is now inside blue circle
blue_circle = None
for n in after['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] > 20:
        blue_circle = n
        break

if blue_circle:
    bc_boundary = blue_circle['boundary']
    bc_min_r = min([p[0] for p in bc_boundary])
    bc_max_r = max([p[0] for p in bc_boundary])
    bc_min_c = min([p[1] for p in bc_boundary])
    bc_max_c = max([p[1] for p in bc_boundary])
    
    for mp in m_positions:
        if mp[0] >= bc_min_r and mp[1] <= bc_max_r and mp[2] >= bc_min_c and mp[3] <= bc_max_c:
            print(f"Magenta square {mp} is inside blue circle!")
        elif mp[1] >= bc_min_r and mp[1] <= bc_max_r and mp[3] >= bc_min_c and mp[3] <= bc_max_c:
            print(f"Magenta square {mp} partially overlaps blue circle")

# Let me also check if there's any other change in the board
# Count all objects
objects = {}
for n in after['nodes']:
    objects.setdefault(n['color'], []).append(n['pixels'])
print(f"Objects: {dict(sorted(objects.items()))}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
